timing_method: warm (compile-once) then median of 150-200 per-call block_until_ready at batch=1
opacity_method: ExoJAX OpaDirect (LPF line-by-line, init_lpf numatrix + vmapped Voigt-Hjerting hjert + line_strength + gamma_exomol + doppler_sigma)
install_path_or_blocker: /tmp/exojax_venv (venv from cflibs-gpu2 py3.12; exojax 2.5.0 via pip; venv CPU jax/jaxlib 0.10.2 UNINSTALLED, gpu2 CUDA jax 0.9.2 reused via PYTHONPATH=/cluster/shared/envs/cflibs-gpu2/lib/python3.12/site-packages). Synthetic exomol-type mdb fed in -> NO VALD/Kurucz download needed.
jacobian_mode: jax.jacfwd (forward-mode, 17 inputs). jacrev OOMs at 4000ch (tries to alloc 17.95 GiB).
est_k3_note: 3 * (forward + jacfwd) Gauss-Newton estimate at 300 lines / 4000 channels
op_count_note: 34 jaxpr eqns / ~16 fused HLO kernels for the forward. Few launches, BUT the dominant fusion is the (300 x 4000) = 1.2M-element Voigt-Hjerting numatrix; jacfwd replicates it across 17 tangents.

roi_sweep_jacfwd.note: median us at batch=1, 300 lines, jacfwd; K3 = 3*(fwd+jacfwd)

reasoning: ExoJAX OpaDirect at the CF-LIBS target (300 lines x 4000 channels) gives forward=390us and jacfwd Jacobian=3386us, so a K=3 Gauss-Newton inversion estimates ~11.3 ms -- 7.3x over the hand-rolled 1.54 ms and far above the <1 ms hot-path budget. The forward op-launch count is FINE (~16 fused kernels, 34 jaxpr eqns), confirming the forward is NOT launch-limited; the killer is the (Nline x Nchan)=1.2M-element Voigt-Hjerting numatrix ExoJAX materializes per line per channel, whose cost the 17-input Jacobian multiplies ~9x. Even the most aggressive trim (100 lines @ 1000 channels) still yields K3 ~ 1.19 ms, and NO measured configuration crossed under 1 ms for K=3. ExoJAX targets high-accuracy OFFLINE opacity/manifold generation (PreMODIT/MODIT + partition-function rigor), not single-shot real-time inversion. Keep the hand-rolled forward on the hot path; use ExoJAX OFFLINE for manifold pre-computation and reference-accuracy cross-checks.
